title: Care and Cleaning of OGI BioReactor pH Probes
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2rhb8","text":"This is the protocol for the care and cleaning of your OGI BioReactor pH Module Probes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7skgr","text":"The probes should be cleaned immediately after an experiment to prevent contamination.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cprkr","text":"The protocol steps should be carried out in quick succession to prevent the probe tip from drying out.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e5t5d","text":"pH probes should always be stored wet, with the tip soaked in electrode storage solution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"7649s","text":"Ensure sterile conditions are maintained all through the protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqkuf","text":"Ensure you have familiarised yourself with the guidelines and warnings for this protocol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"92ic9","text":"Do not touch the electrode glass bulb with fingers or other oily or abrasive objects.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"95btm","text":"DO NOT clean the electrodes by rubbing with a paper towel or cloth as this may build charge and interfere with the pH measurements.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"e7caf","text":"70% ethanol.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8v62o","text":"Ultrapure (18 MOhm) water.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bsn6s","text":"\"Electrode Storage Solution\" - 3M Potassium Chloride (https://www.sentek.co.uk/product/electrode-storage-solution-150ml/, other suppliers are available)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":54,"length":66}],"data":{}},{"key":"7cn9g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"9s38g","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d6lle","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"50vtm","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"971kp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dcf1o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1b4q","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"3D6B078C589A11EF83E00A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.sentek.co.uk/product/electrode-storage-solution-150ml"}},"1":{"type":"equipment","mutability":"IMMUTABLE","data":{"id":5327,"guid":"84E10437B7A911EF86D60A58A9FEAC02","brand":"Sentek","sku":"P600002","name":"pH Probe","link":"www.ogibio.co.uk","type":"Accessory","specifications":"","file":null,"image":null,"vendor":{"id":1872,"name":"ŌGI Bio","link":"https://ogibio.co.uk/","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Visually inspect the probe for trapped air bubbles within the electrolyte solution. If bubbles are present, shake probe in a downward motion to remove them.
Unwrap the parafilm and remove the storage sleeve from the probe.
Rinse the storage sleeve with ultrapure water and shake to remove any residues of water.
Rinse the probe by spraying ultrapure water on the thicker portion and allowing it to run down the lower shaft.
Gently dab dry the probe with a paper towel.
Repeat Step 4 with 70% ethanol.
Insert the pH probe through the flask bung.
Spray the lower protruding glass portion of the probe with 70% ethanol.
Fill the storage sleeve with 70% ethanol.
Pinch the top of the sleeve to temporarily deform it, such that air cannot be trapped inside the sleeve when it is slid onto the electrode's tip.
Slide the deformed sleeve onto the electrode's tip and remove after 1 minute.
Rinse the sleeve with ultrapure water and set aside to dry.
Rinse the lower portion of the pH probe with ultrapure water. It is now ready for an experiment.
Remove the probe from the bung.
Spray ultrapure water on the thicker portion and allowing it to run down the lower shaft. Perform this and other washes over a designated waste bottle.
Repeat Step 15 with 70% ethanol.
Rinse the storage sleeve with ultrapure water and shake to remove excess liquid.
Repeat Steps 9 through 11.
Repeat Steps 15 and 17.
Pipette 100 μL of electrode storage solution into the sleeve.
Pinching the top of the sleeve as in Step 10, slide the sleeve over the end of the pH probe.
Gently dab the probe dry with a paper towel.
Dab away any excess fluid with a paper towel.
Wrap the sleeve with parafilm such that the film secures the end of the sleeve to the glass of the probe. Store upright to prevent the formation of bubbles within the electrolyte solution.
creator: Jack Hocking
